_chevron\_right_

This is a prompter app that automatically scrolls the entered text from bottom to top.

It is often used by TV newscasters to read scripts, and by politicians and speakers to read speeches.

Also known as a teleprompter.

In addition to speeches, you can also display the lyrics like in karaoke when singing your own songs, or automatically show a cue card in place of the assistant director of a TV variety show.

You can fine-tune the text scrolling speed, font size and color, and background color.

While scrolling, you can pause, go back a little, or move forward a little.

Updated on

Aug 28, 2025

Tools [Tools](https://play.google.com/store/apps/category/TOOLS)

![Thumbnail (1920x1080)](https://i.ytimg.com/vi_webp/vbh1L8y4beE/maxresdefault.webp)
# [How to use Microsoft Word as a Teleprompter for free](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vbh1L8y4beE)

**Visibility**: Public
**Uploaded by**: [Jeremy Chapman](http://www.youtube.com/@DeployJeremy)
**Uploaded at**: 2025-04-07T16:32:10-07:00
**Published at**: 2025-04-07T16:32:10-07:00
**Length**: 08:37
**Views**: 17503
**Likes**: 433
**Category**: Howto & Style

## Description

```
Find out how you can use Microsoft Word as a free teleprompter. I'll show you how to convert a document to use with a teleprompter or separate monitor, as well as how to make it automatically scroll for you as you read it aloud. Then I'll give you tips for common mistakes you'll likely make along the way, in addition to pro tips for formatting text to emphasize certain words, know when questions are coming, or present with other people using the same script.

Link to 5" monitor: https://amzn.to/4j2Nj3Q  (Eyoyo brand - does not flip image, retailer affiliate) 
Link to 7" monitor with flipping: https://amzn.to/424s7Vc

Chapters:
00:00 Why use Microsoft Word as a teleprompter
00:50 Starting in Word with a normal document
01:53 Removing pages in Word

02:44 How to make the background black with white text
03:54 How to narrow text to avoid side-to-side eye movement

07:25 Hardware considerations to set up a basic teleprompter at home

#microsoftword #teleprompter
```

## Transcript

So today I'm going to show you how you can use Microsoft Word to easily convert any document into something that works perfectly as a teleprompter. So you can put for example a small 5 to 7 in or so monitor underneath your camera or above your camera even or you can use a proper teleprompter that basically mirrors the image of a monitor and then it projects it back at you right over the top of the lens. Both of them work so that you can read it. So, the thing with teleprompterss though, their software is pretty limited and it can be really expensive, but you can use Word for free even if you use Word on the web with a free Microsoft account or you can use Word on the desktop on PC or Mac and you've got a lot more formatting capabilities than you'll have in even some of the best prompter software out there. And I've used a lot of it. So, here I'm using Word and Windows. And what I'll demonstrate today works more or less the same way if you're using a PC or a Mac. And again, if you're using Word on the web in your browser, you can do that with a free account. So, it's completely free what I'm showing you today. And this is something that you'd only need to set up one time. Basically, you can use the doc and then create that kind of as a template for future use as you write scripts in the future. So, here, for example, I have a script and I've done a little bit of formatting to make every sentence its own paragraph. so that it isn't too large or huge of a unbroken text block. And as I scroll down, you'll see that Word is default set up using something called pages, like for printing, with separation between each of these pages with a header and a footer. But there's an easy fix for that, and I'm going to show you that in a second. Now, the first thing you'll want to do to ensure that the top lines don't scroll by too fast is to add a little space at the top of the document by adding a few blank lines. And I just place the cursor here on the top left. And I hit the enter key half a dozen times or so to place the text around the middle of the page. Now, let's get rid of those separate pages that I showed before. And you'll do that by changing the default page layout to web layout. Now, use this control on the bottom in the right corner of the screen. And it's also in the view tab if you want to use it in the ribbon. And if you're using Word on the web, from its view tab, you'll click separate pages to unseparate them. So now we're using the full width of the page, but the text looks pretty tiny now. So I'll address that by using the built-in zoom control. Now you just need to hold down the control key, then press the plus sign until the text is the size that you want it to be. Of course, you can also change the text size itself if you prefer from kind of the home tab of Word. But um I like to edit with smaller text kind of at 100% zoom and then just make it larger on screen using the control with the plus key later. So now it's looking pretty good. But uh for it to really look like a teleprompter, you'll generally want the background to be black and the text then to be white or a lighter color for contrast. And to do that, I tend to search for the page color control in the search box and then choose black. And you can also navigate to it using the ribbon, using the design tab if you're on Windows or Mac with the desktop client. In the browser version, it's actually in the layout tab using the classic version of the ribbon. Now, for both, you can either choose dark mode or use the page color. Now, I find that the dark mode works better in the web version of Word and the page color better in the desktop apps. That way, it's also backwards compatible if you have older versions of Word on the desktop because dark mode on desktop was only introduced in 2022. So, only the newer clients have that. And now it's starting to look a lot more like a teleprompter. One more minor setting then is to get rid of all the extra Word interface and then put Word in focus mode. Now, using the focus button here on the bottom right, you can do that. Now, it looks a lot more like it and it's still editable, by the way, in line if you want to make edits. Now, the next problem is that depending on how far away you are from the prompter uh with your eyes while they're reading, they might actually move left or right, and you don't want that. So, what you can do actually uh if you're say 3 to 5t away from your camera, in my case, I'm about 5t or 1 and 1/2 mters away from my camera, I can solve for that basically by using the ruler, then hitting control A to select all of my text. And from there, uh you use the the two triangle kind of icons on the left. If you use that small rectangle under those two triangles, then you can actually move the margin over on the left hand side. And once you get it where you want it, you can move over to the other side of the page on the right hand side. And just by using the triangle that goes upwards, you can then slide that over to the right until you're more or less under the lens of your camera. So for me now, it's directly under my lens. And even at close range, my eyes shouldn't be moving left and right on the screen. So, the last thing that you're going to want to figure out is how to scroll the text in the document. So, I actually prefer manually scrolling, and I've even tried AI based autoscrolling apps, but I prefer to manually scroll using a scroll wheel or another person if you actually have a volunteer to scroll for you. Now, there is an automated way though to do this inside of Word by basically clicking down on the scroll wheel of a mouse and you'll see an icon like this one. Then moving your cursor down from the icon, you can move it further down to make it go faster or move it up to make it go slower or move it above the icon to make it go the other way. And if you do this approach by yourself, you're going to want to practice and find the pace and the speed that you want. All right, so let's try it out. So, why don't we first look at how it works? So, I have a JPEG here, and it doesn't have a transparent background. Now I'm going to use this button to remove the background. And you'll see that it just takes a second. And now the background's gone. Now I can read a solid background layer if I want with a different color or whatever I want. And this was all baked into Windows. Okay. So now we have a working teleprompter in Word that can even autoscroll if we want it to. But don't click away just yet because inevitably you'll hit a few common issues by doing this. The first one that I see the most is when text disappears from the page. Now, this is usually because the text is configured to be black and the page color is also black. So, of course, it becomes invisible. Now, the way to fix this and let you move between black and white page colors if you want to to edit, for example, is by defining the text color as automatic, not black, not white, but automatic like you're seeing here. Another option is to use contrasting colors that work on both light and dark page backgrounds. For example, this cyan blue color. Now, let me give you a few pro tips because I've literally filmed more than a thousand videos with a prompter. And if I want to punch a word at a sentence, for example, I might bold it or underline it so that I know which words to really add emphasis on. Also, for sentences that need to be phrased as a question, a good trick that I stole from the Spanish language is to add the question mark to the beginning of the sentence. Then you know right away that you need to intonate that sentence like a question. Then for multiple people that are reading from the same script, another good trick is to bold their names, then use a highlighter color with their name so that each person can clearly see who's supposed to deliver each line. And then you'll just want to make sure that you use a different but consistent color for each speaker. Finally, in terms of hardware, the cheapest option that works really well that I'm using actually right now is by connecting a 5- in USB powered monitor underneath your lens. There are also ways to do this using a capture card that goes into your phone, for example, then using your phone's camera app because the capture card basically mimics a USB webcam. And this one though that I've got here, this monitor was about $60. I'll put a link in the description. And it's mounted using a cell phone mount uh on a clip on a monopod in my case. And if you want to use a small or a proper teleprompter like I've got here with a padcaster, uh then you're going to make sure that the monitor itself can support screen mirroring or flipping like my fieldworld monitor here. It's a 7-in monitor, a little bit bigger than is required for this Padcaster, but it will flip the other way. And both of these options are pretty inexpensive. Both were under $100. So, hopefully these tips help you in terms of transforming Word into a teleprompter. Now, if you like the tip, be sure to give me a like. Otherwise, subscribe to my channel for more hardware reviews and other software tips. And we'll see you again soon.

data.description: Teleprompter software allows you to read your lines quickly and without mistakes. Choose between automatic scrolling and voice-activated scrolling, which ...
data.markdown:
Teleprompter software allows you to read your lines _quickly and without mistakes_. Choose between automatic scrolling and voice-activated scrolling, which follows your voice as you talk.


HOW TO USE: Simply copy/paste your script into this window, adjust the font size and speed, and press the play button  at the top.   If you're reading your script on a teleprompter, the 'Horizontal Flip'  button at the top flips your script to a mirror image.   Keep reading to learn all about the software or click the instructions icon  above for videos and manuals.


UPDATES: This version was released on August 7th, 2024. We're constantly updating the code to add new features, fix bugs, and provide instructions.


FREE TELEPROMPTER: The software is free, with no trial periods, upgrades, or limitations.   If you need help or have feature requests, simply email support@telepromptermirror.com (don't forget to check out our instructions  at the top first!).


LANGUAGE SUPPORT: Multilingual support--you can use non-English characters, even Kanji.


VIDEO INSTRUCTIONS: Click the instructions link at the top of the screen for our complete guide.


MOBILE DEVICES: On mobile devices, press the 'Menu' icon  to access the controls.


BOOKMARK THIS PAGE: In case you need to return to this page for teleprompting, bookmark this page (CTRL+D).


FONT COLORS & IMAGES: Font color and images are enabled by clicking on the  icon at the top for rich text copy/pasting.   With this mode on, you can write your teleprompter script in another program such as Word or another editor, then copy/paste it into this screen with your formatting preserved.


ZOOM TELEPROMPTER: In addition to recording videos and making speeches, teleprompter software can be used with Zoom, Microsoft Teams, Skype, and other meeting software to read your notes while making direct eye contact.   By outlining what you're going to say in advance, you can streamline your meetings and deliver accurate, detailed information.


WEB BROWSER TELEPROMPTER: It runs in your browser, so there's no need to download or install anything.   If you prefer to download teleprompter software, check out TeleprompterMirror.com/software/


TABLETS, CELL PHONES, & LAPTOPS: In addition to running on a desktop PC, the software automatically adjusts to your screen size on smaller devices.   When editing your script on cell phones and tablets, you'll find it helpful to sync a bluetooth keyboard or plug it into the USB port of your cell phone, tablet, or iPad.   Adding a touchpad keyboard or mouse makes it even easier to edit your script.


VOICE ACTIVATED SCROLLING: Activate it in the Chrome browser by clicking the  microphone button above.   The voice activated software is a separate webpage.   You can also copy/paste your script there.


PRIVACY & SECURITY: Text is only stored locally on your web browser, so scripts aren't uploaded to a server.


HOW TO LOAD SCRIPTS: Copying/pasting can be done in Windows with CTRL+C / CTRL+V and on Mac with Command+C / Command+V. Looking to paste as plain text with no formatting? In Windows, the hotkey is Ctrl+Alt+V and on Mac it's Option+Shift+Command+V. Alternatively, you can also right click and select paste as plain text.


EDITING & SAVING: You can edit your scripts on this page, or use copy/paste to load them from another program.   Use the save and load buttons to save it to your computer for later.


TELEPROMPTER PRACTICE: Need teleprompter practice? Press play right now to start practicing with this pre-written script.   You can adjust the font size and speed to practice your teleprompting.


SPEED: Set the speed to a comfortable pace or click the microphone icon above for voice activated scrolling.


MIRROR FLIP: Click the icon above to mirror flip your text (handy when using beamsplitter mirrors). Looking for a teleprompter or beamsplitter mirror? Check out our website, TeleprompterMirror.com


OPERATING SYSTEMS: Works on Windows, Mac, iPad, Android, Linux, and more.   If you're using an iPad or Android tablet, add a shortcut to your home screen to enable full screen.



KEYBOARD SHORTCUTS: Here are some useful keyboard shortcuts to control your teleprompter:


Up: Increases Font Size


Down: Decreases Font Size


Left: Slows Down Teleprompter


Right: Speeds Up Teleprompter


Space: Starts / Stops Teleprompter


Escape: Rewinds your script back to the start


F11: Fullscreen Browser (Fn+F11 on compact keyboards)


Having trouble with the keyboard shortcuts? Don't forget to use your mouse or touch screen to click on the script once it's running to allow keyboard input to the screen.


TIPS: The voice activated and non-voice activated versions of this software are actually two separate pages, so if you're switching between them, you'll simply need to copy and paste your script.


READY? START TELEPROMPTING!

ABOUT US: And now, a message from our sponsors--us! A lot of you have been asking how I've recorded hundreds of YouTube videos.   Right now, I’m standing in front of a chromakey backdrop.   By combining a green screen with a teleprompter, I’m able to script all my lines in advance, adding the visuals in post processing.


I'm going to let you in on a secret: a teleprompter is simply a beamsplitter mirror that displays my script in front of a camera.   Using a teleprompter allows me to thoroughly research detailed scripts in advance and deliver them effortlessly.


The magic is in the highly transparent mirror, which provides tint-free visibility for my camera to record through.   Because my script is in front of the camera lens, I can keep direct eye contact with my audience.


Hi, I'm Krista, videographer, actress, and anything else I want to be when using my teleprompter! I made this program to help you deliver your lines quickly and confidently on camera.


I hope you enjoy it and find it useful for sharing your message with the world!



DOWNLOAD TELEPROMPTER SOFTWARE:

Looking to download teleprompter software? We offer a few different resources for you.   Check out our reviews of other developers teleprompter software at [https://telepromptermirror.com/software/](https://telepromptermirror.com/software/)

If you're looking for a beta version for Mac & PC of this software, you can download it from us at https://telepromptermirror.com/teleprompter-downloads/


Looking to buy a teleprompter or teleprompter glass? Check out our website telepromptermirror.com


Looking to build a professional DIY teleprompter? We've written step-by-step instructions for you at: https://youtu.be/qnhIuDGCmNs


Do you have a feature you'd like to see added? A bug which you'd like to report? Write us your suggestions at support@telepromptermirror.com

data.markdown:
# CuePrompter the \#1 Free Online Teleprompter

![](https://cueprompter.com/images/background-design-1.svg)

CuePrompter is a free teleprompter/autocue service.
Your browser works like a teleprompter and there is no extra software needed.
Free for any use (both commercial and non-commercial).
Bookmark this site and come again whenever you need teleprompter services.


![](https://cueprompter.com/images/background-design-3.svg)

💡 Need help writing your script? Try our
[AI Script Generator](https://www.teleprompter.com/tools/script-generator)
for professional video scripts in seconds


![](https://cueprompter.com/images/background-design-grey-2.svg)

Start new prompter

Start old prompter

## What You Get with CuePrompter

### No signup, no downloads

Works instantly in your browser. Just paste your script and go.

### Adjustable scroll speed

Control how fast the text scrolls to match your natural speaking pace.

### Mirror mode

Flip the text for use with physical teleprompter glass and beam splitter setups.

### Multiple color themes

Choose from white on black, black on white, or yellow on black for comfortable reading.

### Works on any device

Use it on your phone, tablet, laptop, or desktop. Any modern browser works.

### Free for any use

100% free for both commercial and non-commercial projects. No watermarks, no limits.

![Women recording with teleprompter](https://cueprompter.com/images/women-recording-with-teleprompter.webp)

![](https://cueprompter.com/images/background-design-grey-3.svg)

## Ready for More?

Take your scripts to the next level with [Teleprompter.com](https://teleprompter.com/).
Get voice-activated scrolling, 4K video recording, cloud script sync, and remote control from your iPhone or Apple Watch.
Available on iOS, Android, macOS, and Web.


![Women recording with teleprompter](https://cueprompter.com/images/women-recording-with-teleprompter.webp)

[Try Teleprompter.com Free](https://teleprompter.com/)

[![Download on App Store](https://cueprompter.com/images/download-on-the-app-store.svg)](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/teleprompter/id1420515755)

[![Get it on Google Play](https://cueprompter.com/images/get-it-on-google-play.svg)](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.mkapp.teleprompter)

![star](https://cueprompter.com/images/star.svg)![star](https://cueprompter.com/images/star.svg)![star](https://cueprompter.com/images/star.svg)![star](https://cueprompter.com/images/star.svg)![star](https://cueprompter.com/images/star.svg)

Rated five stars by 20.000+ creators


## Frequently Asked Questions

What is a teleprompter?

A teleprompter (also called an autocue) is a device or application that displays scrolling text so a speaker can read a script while looking directly at the camera or audience. CuePrompter turns your browser into a free teleprompter — no extra hardware or software needed.

Is CuePrompter really free?

Yes, CuePrompter is 100% free for both commercial and non-commercial use. Just paste your script and start scrolling. No account, no downloads, no watermarks.

Can I use CuePrompter on my phone or tablet?

Yes, CuePrompter works in any modern browser on smartphones, tablets, laptops, and desktops. For an even better mobile experience with features like voice-activated scrolling and 4K recording, try [Teleprompter.com](https://teleprompter.com/) available on [iOS](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/teleprompter/id1420515755) and [Android](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.mkapp.teleprompter).

How do I use a teleprompter for YouTube videos?

Place your device with CuePrompter running just below or beside your camera lens. Paste your script, adjust the scroll speed, and start recording. This lets you maintain eye contact with your audience while reading your script naturally. For a dedicated setup, check out our guide on [using a teleprompter for YouTube](https://cueprompter.com/teleprompter-for-youtube).

What is the difference between CuePrompter and Teleprompter.com?

CuePrompter is a free browser-based teleprompter for quick script reading. [Teleprompter.com](https://teleprompter.com/) adds professional features like voice-activated scrolling, 4K video recording, cloud script sync, remote control from iPhone or Apple Watch, and multi-platform live streaming to Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, and LinkedIn.

data.markdown:
[Skip to main content](https://support.google.com/chrome/answer/14218344?hl=en#search-form)

# Use Reading mode in Chrome

Customize your reading experience on Chrome with Reading mode. This feature helps you:

- Focus more easily on text.
- Reduce distractions from images and videos on screen.
- Select alternate typeface and font size.
- Choose color theme.

If you’re using Chrome on a computer, you can also:

- Listen to the text you read.
- Select a voice and change voice speed.
- Adjust line and letter spacing.

ComputerAndroidiPhone & iPad

More

More

## Turn on Reading mode

**Important:** These settings only apply to the text you want to read in the side panel. They won’t apply to any other content in Chrome or to general websites.

1. On your computer, open Chrome.
2. Go to a website with text that you’d like to read.
3. Right-click the page.
4. Select **Open in reading mode**.

**Tips:**

- To make the panel bigger, slide the panel border to the left.
- To pin Reading mode, at the top right of the side panel, select Pin to toolbar ![Pin](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/rhNF1LmJVlKlcgd01yrqjf9t44VxEs6oH9VWc8yW9dH1s4xjpz3iObN757EjyOrbjBhC=h36).
- At the top right of the toolbar, you can access Reading mode after you pin it. Select Reading mode ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/ze8Mb3FF8pfNJZjjCcpUdWsnV6WcNr0zNGc4).

## Use Reading mode to make reading easier

When you use Reading mode, you can adjust font settings for easier reading.

In the toolbar, you can:

- **Adjust the font size:** Select Font size ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/EKTWrYJjeCi2ReT17P9AIvNs1D1HCQkdnCqE).


  - **To decrease the font size:** Select Decrease ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/jlklFA4kIUqhBD5KmRlyy3SKOASOAi1cr8YQ).
  - **To increase the font size:** Select Increase ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/3L9MaG3lL0RwLK64MqN1j3spPeRe1IYKiyAt).
  - **To reset to default font size:** Select **Reset**.
- **Adjust the font:** Select Font ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/d9rijYFWQrbvpdvCpOeY9gqKnuapfZJbMCPi).
- **Change the Reading mode color theme:** Select Color theme ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/X4YtCkhwGVxeVRZRoqqyJJi5KrZ4PwKSVJb0).
- **Change the spacing between lines:** Select Line spacing ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/aX5uxnN7Hwbg0Ig2NLEUD7eLopGeVCV2ta84).
- **Change the spacing between letters:** Select Letter spacing ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/uCfHbDoum7Fz7CpL0YKKlmphPNceO2auaeg3).

## Listen to the text you read with read aloud

When you use Reading mode, you can listen to the text on a website.

To turn on read aloud and listen to the text on a website, under "Reading mode," select Play ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/U1ADE6y46BJw8yWmqtqrKWEo9NUB2fQrXAPD).

While in read aloud, use the toolbar to:

- **Play and pause while you read:** Select Play ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/U1ADE6y46BJw8yWmqtqrKWEo9NUB2fQrXAPD) or Pause ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/A8xPsXt7DrrS9cTHeGVRFq2RBWotiU8x6hVg).
- **Jump to the beginning of the previous or next sentence:** Select Previous ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/3REPCkAidQUNTWOsBlgR9SfbFTUebGt4Kjfm) or Next ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/Hwr66SGXsOBJJlhXLlJRBNiA2XwB26jeKZlt).
- **Adjust the playback speed:** Select Playback speed ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/SlZB9s2UQ3Gr3ArK8f11n9UcQ9DozdCXWnnD).
- **Select your preferred voice:**
  - **To preview a voice:**
    - **Through the toolbar:**
      1. Select Voice ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/pd58NT3J24oTW9zI88Zu1BWSMZXfhbJncvPy).
      2. Next to the voice name, select Preview ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/U1ADE6y46BJw8yWmqtqrKWEo9NUB2fQrXAPD).
    - **Through your keyboard:**
      1. To put keyboard focus on the voice you want to preview, in the menu, press **Arrow up** and **Arrow down**.
      2. Press Right arrow ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/Hwr66SGXsOBJJlhXLlJRBNiA2XwB26jeKZlt)![and then](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/3_l97rr0GvhSP2XV5OoCkV2ZDTIisAOczrSdzNCBxhIKWrjXjHucxNwocghoUa39gw=h36)**Enter**.
  - **To select a voice:** Select the voice you prefer.
  - **To add a language:** Select Voice ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/pd58NT3J24oTW9zI88Zu1BWSMZXfhbJncvPy)![and then](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/3_l97rr0GvhSP2XV5OoCkV2ZDTIisAOczrSdzNCBxhIKWrjXjHucxNwocghoUa39gw=h36)**Languages...**.

    - **To search a language:** At the search bar, enter the language.
    - **To add a language:** Turn on the preferred language.
    - **To remove a language:** Turn off the preferred language.
- **Select your preferred highlighting mode:**
1. Select **Voice highlight**.
2. **To turn on text highlighting:**From the dropdown menu, select one of the following:

     - Adaptive highlighting
     - Word
     - Sentence
3. **To turn off text highlighting:** From the dropdown menu, select **Off**.

**Tips:**

- When there isn’t enough space for the toolbar options to fit, you can find the toolbar options when you select More ![More](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/E2q6Vj9j60Dw0Z6NZFEx5vSB9yoZJp7C8suuvQXVA_2weMCXstGD7JEvNrzX3wuQrPtL=h36).
- Reading mode automatically downloads Google natural voices for your current language. If download fails, the system voice for your language will be used instead.

## Using screen readers without read aloud

If you’re using a screen reader, you can access the text content in the side panel.

To navigate through the text content:

- **MacOS: VO** \+ **Left Arrow** or **VO** \+ **Right Arrow**
- **Windows or Linux: Up Arrow** or **Down Arrow** or **Alt** \+ **Up Arrow** or **Alt** + **Down Arrow**
- **ChromeOS:** **Search** \+ **Up Arrow** or **Search** + **Down Arrow**

- [Use magnification on your screen](https://support.google.com/accessibility/android/answer/6006949)
- [Change text & display settings](https://support.google.com/accessibility/android/answer/11183305)
- [Manage Chrome side panel](https://support.google.com/chrome/answer/13156494)
- [Use Reading mode & Read aloud on your Chromebook](https://support.google.com/chromebook/answer/15284142)

In this video, I show you how to make Microsoft Word scroll documents like a teleprompter, but you have to know how to use it! In this video, I'll show you how to use Microsoft Word as a teleprompter. This includes:
How to make your script scroll up the screen.
How to remove the gaps between pages.
How to confine the text so you don't need to move your eyes.
And how to position your webcam or camera beside the text.

Teleprompters are used in a lot of professional settings, and they can really help to improve the look and feel of your presentation, whether it is to video or a crowd of people. By following my tips, you can make your presentation look natural, flawless, and professional!
